author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
aruṇa⋅kamala⋅dale, śeṣa vichāiba,
basāiba kiśora⋅kiśorī
alakā⋅āvṛta mukha⋅ paṅkaja manohara,
marakata⋅śyāma hema⋅gaurī

prāṇeśvari! kabe more habe kṛpādiṭhi
ājñāya āniyā kabe, vividha phulabara,
śunaba vacana du̐hu miṭhi

mṛgamada⋅tilaka, sasindūra banāyaba,
lepaba candana⋅gandhe
gā̐thi’ mālatīphula, hāra pahirāoba,
dhāoyāba madhukaravṛnde

lalitā kabe more, jīvana deoyaba,
vījana māruta manda
śramajala sakala, miṭaba du̐hu⋅kalevara,
heraba parama ānande

narottama⋅dāsa⋅, āśa padapaṅkaja,
sevana mādhurīpāne
hooyaba hena dina, nā dekhiye kona cihna,
du̐hujana heraba nayāne
en_translation:
Upon red lotus petals I will spread a bed and seat the youthful Divine Couple. Their captivating lotus-faces are adorned with wavy hair—Kṛṣṇa dark as emerald, and Rādhā golden as pure gold.

O Mistress of my life! When will Your merciful glance fall upon me? When, by Your command, will I bring various excellent flowers and hear the sweet words of both of You?

I will apply musk tilaka made with vermilion and anoint You both with fragrant sandalwood paste. Stringing jasmine flowers, I will offer garlands, making swarms of bees chase after You.

When will Lalitā grant me the service of fanning You both with a gentle breeze, so that all perspiration will vanish from Your bodies and I will behold You with supreme joy?

Narottama dāsa hopes to drink the sweetness of service at Your lotus feet. Though I see no sign of such a day arriving, I long to behold both of You with my own eyes.
